definition.pre_v1: All governed services remain pre-v1 while the product is exploratory, internally evaluated, or visually/operationally unstable.
definition.v1_0: A governed service may be called v1.0 only when market usefulness is evidenced and the human architect is using it as an active business instrument.

notes: The service must demonstrate external usefulness beyond internal curiosity.
Examples include paid use, retained external users, or explicit problem-solution confirmation.
The human architect must actively use the service in real business work.
A personal experiment or dormant deployment does not qualify.
The service must pass a production-lane review with no unresolved high-risk deterministic failures.

promotion_flow: Collect evidence hashes for market-proof, operator-business-use, and production-stability.
Confirm the service has operated through the required stability window.
Issue a release-review verdict referencing the evidence hashes.
Only then may the service version be promoted to 1.0.
anti_patterns: Declaring v1 because the implementation feels complete without business use.
Declaring v1 from internal enthusiasm alone.
Declaring v1 while market proof exists but the human architect does not actively rely on the service.
